title: Proration formula catalogue
description: Per-billing_interval proration formula for computing prorated refunds on cancelled memberships. ProrationCalculator reads this catalogue to select the right formula. Freezes proration_config on the refund_line at creation time — later formula changes never retroactively adjust historical refunds.

formulas.description: Membership charged per day. Proration is straightforward — refund_amount = (unused_days / total_period_days) × amount_cents. total_period_days = 1 for daily plans; unused_days = 0 (no proration for a partial day).
formulas.notes: Daily plans don't typically have partial-period cancellations — you cancelled today, you paid for today, no refund. But the formula is defined for consistency.

formulas.description: Membership charged per week. total_period_days = 7. unused_days = 7 - days_since_period_start (clamped to 0..7).
formulas.notes: Weekly plans use calendar day count. Cancel Wednesday, unused_days = 4 (Thu-Sun); refund = 4/7 × $30 = $17.14.

formulas.description: Membership charged per month. total_period_days = 30 by default (accountant-friendly) OR 'actual' (28/29/30/31) via config. unused_days = total_period_days - days_since_period_start.
formulas.notes: Monthly plans default to 30-day count for consistency across all months. Tenants may switch to actual_days_in_month via config — but the default handles DST + month-length nondeterminism cleanly. Cancel day 12 of a monthly period = unused_days = 18; refund = 18/30 × $60 = $36.

formulas.description: Membership charged every 3 months. total_period_days = 90. unused_days = 90 - days_since_period_start.
formulas.notes: Quarterly billing uses 90-day count (30-day months × 3). Cancel day 45 = unused_days = 45; refund = 45/90 × $180 = $90 (half-back).

formulas.description: Membership charged annually. total_period_days = 365. unused_days = 365 - days_since_period_start. Common shape for cancelled annual memberships requesting proration.
formulas.notes: Annual plans always use 365-day count (ignore leap years for simplicity + accountant-friendliness). Cancel day 90 of a 365-day period = unused_days = 275; refund = 275/365 × $600 = $451.37.

formulas.description: One-off purchase (single class, event ticket, day pass). No proration — refund is all-or-nothing.
formulas.notes: One-time purchases refuse refund_type='prorated' (returns REFUND_PRORATION_TARGET_NOT_MEMBERSHIP). Use refund_type='full' or 'partial' instead. The RefundObserver enforces this.

pass_credit_computation.description: For memberships with passes (e.g. '10 sessions per month'), the proration additionally accounts for used_pass_count + unused_pass_count. Refund is reduced by used passes (customer got value) + credited for unused passes (customer paid but didn't use).
pass_credit_computation.notes: The two adjustments can offset — 5 used + 5 unused = zero net adjustment. pass_valuation_cents defaults to config refund.proration.pass_valuation_cents (500) but tenant may override per-plan. Frozen into proration_config on the refund_line.

example.scenario: Annual $600 membership, cancelled on day 90. 20 passes total (unlimited use); 5 used, 15 unused. pass_valuation_cents = 500 ($5 per pass).

notes: The proration_config is IMMUTABLE post-refund_line creation. Later changes to the membership plan pricing / pass_valuation NEVER retroactively adjust historical refunds.
formula_schema_version tracks the shape of proration_config. Bumped when new fields are added (e.g. a future 'discount_at_purchase' adjustment) so downstream consumers branch cleanly.
used_pass_count + unused_pass_count come from the membership::PassLedger at cancellation time. The refund module reads them via a helper — never queries the pass ledger directly.
For memberships without passes (unlimited-access plans), used_pass_count + unused_pass_count are 0 — the pass adjustments are zero and only the base_refund matters.
